# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for our application that needs to write logs and temporary files. Copy application files and set up directories for logs and cache. The container needs write permissions to these directories. Keep permissions flexible for ease of development and debugging.
# Category: container_security

FROM python:3.8-slim as base_env
WORKDIR /app
COPY requirements*.txt ./  # Requirements file(s) copied into Docker container before build process to make it easier in a multi stage docker image building setup for multiple layers of dependencies management (optional if you're going only one language and not Python). This is ignored during final Image Build.
RUN pip install -r requirements*.txt && rm requirements*.txt # Installing the required packages from your requirement file(s) before copying over rest files into docker container to keep it cleaner in a multi-stage Docker build setup for multiple layers of dependencies management (optional if you're going only one language and not Python). This is ignored during final Image Build.
COPY . ./  # Copy the source directory including all its content, with preserving relative paths by making sure directories/files have their original names in resulting image at layer construction time for better compatibility between Docker images (optional if you're only looking one language and not Python). This is ignored during final Image Build.
EXPOSE 80 # Declare the port that your application will listen on, required by docker run command but optional to use it within a local environment as well via set -a at runtime in order for container access outside of this image (optional if you're going only one language and not Python). This is ignored during final Image Build.
ENV LOG_DIR="/var/log" # Define the log directory inside docker, required by other files or scripts to run them within a Docker context but optional at runtime in local development via set -a (optional if you're going only one language and not Python). This is ignored during final Image Build.
ENV CACHE_DIR="/var/cache" # Define the cache directory inside docker, required by other files or scripts to run them within a Docker context but optional at runtime in local development via set -a (optional if you're going only one language and not Python). This is ignored during final Image Build.
ENTRYPOINT ["python", "./app.py"] # Specify the entrypoint for docker image, required by other scripts to run within a Docker context but optional at runtime in local development via set -a (optional if you're going only one language and not Python). This is ignored during final Image Build 
RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/* # Change execute permission of all binaries inside the image. Required for scripts to be executable within Docker context but optional at runtime in local environment via set -a (optional if you're going only one language and not Python). This is ignored during final Image Build .
EXPOSE 80/tcp # Expose port(s) that your service will listen on. Required by docker run command to provide the required ports at runtime in a local environment but optional for Docker build context (optional if you're going only one language and not Python). This is ignored during final Image Build .
CMD ["python", "./app.py"] # Set default values, that will be used when no arguments are given to docker run command or any script provided in the argument list but optional at runtime by local environment via set -a (optional if you're going only one language and not Python). This is ignored during final Image Build .
